primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité, en comptes consolidés, d’un dividende intragroupe / de la créance intragroupe correspondante comme élément couvert au titre du risque de change dans une relation de couverture

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the required precedence rule by selecting the single governing lineage that directly addresses hedge-accounting eligibility questions; IAS 39 directly governs whether intragroup items can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements, while IAS 21 only explains the foreign-exchange effects that survive consolidation.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IAS 39 is the only document in the provided context that directly sets the hedge-accounting eligibility rules for intragroup items in consolidation.

primary_authority.reason: It directly governs whether intragroup items can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements and states the specific foreign-currency exceptions.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs frame the available hedge-accounting models and the requirement that hedging instruments and hedged items generally involve external parties, subject to specified foreign-currency exceptions.
It clarifies why foreign-exchange differences on intragroup monetary items between entities with different functional currencies are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
It supports the distinct net-investment hedge model and expressly limits its guidance to net investment hedges, preventing analogy to other hedge types.

peripheral_authority.reason: It explains general elimination mechanics in consolidation but does not identify hedge-accounting models or eligibility for intragroup FX hedges.
It addresses related-party disclosures and elimination of intragroup balances, not hedge-accounting approaches.
These paragraphs address foreign-currency translation generally, not the hedge-accounting approach set for intragroup dividend-related FX exposure in consolidation.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models available for foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements
Special treatment for intragroup foreign currency exposures that are not fully eliminated on consolidation
Separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on net investments in foreign operations

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge model relevant to recognised foreign-currency exposures, including recognised items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate top-level hedge model, including for qualifying foreign-currency exposures such as forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRIC 16 identify net investment hedging as a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign-currency exposure in consolidation.
